arguments.statement: Facade cladding = non-combustible mineral rainscreen (A1 mineral-core panels, Class 0 outer layers per Cl 3.15)
arguments.rationale: The building is 95 m (over 15 m), so SCDF Fire Code 2023 Cl 3.5.1 mandates wholly non-combustible external walls; this is a compliance requirement, not a preference.
assumptions: combustible external cladding is prohibited over 15 m
author: K. Lim (design QP / architect)
M. Ong (fire engineer)

arguments.statement: Swap the facade cladding to polyethylene-core aluminium composite panel (PE-core ACP) under the 'or equivalent' clause
arguments.rationale: The cost plan is over budget and the facade is the biggest soft target; the PE-core ACP is visually identical and the largest single saving on the package.
assumptions: PE-core ACP is an acceptable equivalent to the specified mineral rainscreen
author: T. Chua (main contractor, commercial)

text: The contractor's position is that the PE-core ACP is visually identical to the specified rainscreen, is covered by the 'or equivalent' clause, and delivers the largest single saving available on the facade package.

The proposed swap breaks D-001's recorded premise that combustible external cladding is prohibited: a polyethylene core is combustible, so it cannot be an 'equivalent' under SCDF Fire Code 2023 Cl 3.5.1 for a 95 m building.

The substitution is rejected. However material the saving, a PE-core panel is combustible and cannot satisfy Cl 3.5.1's requirement that external walls above 15 m be wholly non-combustible; adopting it would falsify the recorded premise of decision D-001 and expose the named QP to personal liability under Building Control Act s.9. The commercial case was heard and does not displace a statutory requirement.

The facade must remain the non-combustible mineral rainscreen because the 95 m tower exceeds 15 m and SCDF Cl 3.5.1 mandates it (D-001). It cannot simply be changed: the cheaper PE-core ACP swap was already proposed and rejected on 3 Mar (D-002), so any change would need to re-open fire-safety approval.
